message:
docs: add .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/ (br-frankenpandas-3xtv)

Five YAML issue-form templates + config.yml structuring inbound
public issues at filing time so triage is faster + reports arrive
with the info we need.

Templates:
- `config.yml`: blank_issues_enabled=false (forces a template choice),
  contact_links for SECURITY.md (security issues → private channel)
  and AGENTS.md (agents → br, not GH issues).
- `bug_report.yml`: crate dropdown, version, reproducer, expected,
  actual, precheck checkboxes (DISCREPANCIES.md review + br search
  + version pinning).
- `feature_request.yml`: pandas method name + pandas docs URL
  (required fields — frankenpandas tracks pandas parity, so every
  feature request maps to a pandas API), example snippet, optional
  Rust signature sketch.
- `conformance_divergence.yml`: the third category specifically
  for pandas-output-differs reports. Routes via DISCREPANCIES.md
  rather than the bug tracker. Carries MUST/SHOULD/MAY dropdown
  (br-nato classification) and pandas-version pin from
  oracle/requirements.txt (br-boyr / br-8irq lineage).
- `perf_regression.yml`: operation-class dropdown aligned with
  br-ing6 perf-baselines + br-xha7 differential-vs-pandas bench
  work. Carries bisect fields + hardware section.

All templates use GitHub issue-forms (YAML with structured body),
not the older markdown-template format. Provides per-field
validation + field-type hints.

Zero source changes. Zero CI changes.

Related:
- br-6d5s (CODEOWNERS / PR template / CONTRIBUTING.md umbrella) —
  3xtv was explicitly listed as a deferred slice under 6d5s;
  landing separately because the template structure differs
  substantially from PR templates.
- br-8k1i (SECURITY.md) — config.yml's contact_links route
  security reports to the private channel 8k1i established.
- br-nato (RequirementLevel MUST/SHOULD/MAY) — the
  conformance_divergence template uses nato's taxonomy.
- br-boyr (pandas-version pin) — the conformance template
  requires users state the pandas version they compared against.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,73 @@
+name: Bug Report
+description: Report a defect in shipped frankenpandas code (not a pandas-parity divergence — use the Conformance template for that)
+title: "[BUG] "
+labels: ["bug", "triage"]
+body:
+  - type: markdown
+    attributes:
+      value: |
+        Thanks for reporting a bug. Please fill in every field — incomplete reports often bounce.
+
+        If this is a pandas-behavior divergence rather than an outright crash, use the Conformance Divergence template instead.
+
+  - type: dropdown
+    id: crate
+    attributes:
+      label: Affected crate
+      options:
+        - fp-frame (DataFrame / Series core)
+        - fp-io (CSV / JSON / Parquet / Excel / Feather / Arrow IPC / SQL)
+        - fp-groupby
+        - fp-join
+        - fp-expr (eval / query / parse)
+        - fp-index (Index / MultiIndex)
+        - fp-runtime (RuntimePolicy / EvidenceLedger)
+        - fp-conformance (harness / fixtures / oracle)
+        - fp-types (Scalar / DType / NullKind)
+        - fp-columnar
+        - frankenpandas (umbrella prelude)
+        - Unsure
+    validations:
+      required: true
+
+  - type: input
+    id: version
+    attributes:
+      label: frankenpandas version
+      placeholder: "0.1.0 (or git SHA for pre-release)"
+    validations:
+      required: true
+
+  - type: textarea
+    id: reproducer
+    attributes:
+      label: Minimal reproducer
+      description: Smallest Rust program that triggers the bug. Include `use` lines and any fixture data inline.
+      render: rust
+    validations:
+      required: true
+
+  - type: textarea
+    id: expected
+    attributes:
+      label: Expected behavior
+      description: What should have happened. Link pandas docs if the expectation comes from pandas parity.
+    validations:
+      required: true
+
+  - type: textarea
+    id: actual
+    attributes:
+      label: Actual behavior
+      description: What happened instead. Include panic message / error text / diff.
+    validations:
+      required: true
+
+  - type: checkboxes
+    id: precheck
+    attributes:
+      label: Pre-filing checks
+      options:
+        - label: I have checked `crates/fp-conformance/DISCREPANCIES.md` for a documented intentional divergence that matches my observation.
+        - label: I have searched the bead tracker (`br list --status open`) for an existing frankenpandas-* bead covering this behavior.
+        - label: My reproducer uses the latest published crate version (or a pinned git SHA).

@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+blank_issues_enabled: false
+contact_links:
+  - name: Security vulnerability report
+    url: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankenpandas/security/advisories/new
+    about: |
+      Private disclosure for security issues. DO NOT file public issues
+      for vulnerabilities. See SECURITY.md for SLA + scope.
+  - name: Agent swarm bead tracker
+    url: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankenpandas/blob/main/AGENTS.md
+    about: |
+      If you are an AI agent in the frankenpandas swarm, file beads via
+      `br create --no-db --no-auto-flush --no-auto-import` rather than
+      GitHub issues. See AGENTS.md for the workflow.

@@ -0,0 +1,81 @@
+name: Conformance Divergence
+description: Report that frankenpandas produces a different result than pandas on an input where we claim parity
+title: "[CONFORMANCE] "
+labels: ["conformance", "triage"]
+body:
+  - type: markdown
+    attributes:
+      value: |
+        Conformance divergences are specially triaged — they may land as:
+          - A fix (our bug).
+          - A DISCREPANCIES.md entry (intentional deviation, documented).
+          - A packet fixture backport (we test the new case).
+
+        Please don't use this template for outright crashes — use the Bug Report template for those.
+
+  - type: input
+    id: pandas_method
+    attributes:
+      label: pandas method
+      placeholder: "df.groupby([k1, k2]).sum() / series.rolling(3).std() / ..."
+    validations:
+      required: true
+
+  - type: textarea
+    id: input
+    attributes:
+      label: Input DataFrame / Series
+      description: Reproducible construction in pandas syntax (we'll translate to Rust)
+      render: python
+    validations:
+      required: true
+
+  - type: textarea
+    id: pandas_output
+    attributes:
+      label: pandas output
+      description: `repr()` or shape summary of what pandas produces
+      render: text
+    validations:
+      required: true
+
+  - type: textarea
+    id: frankenpandas_output
+    attributes:
+      label: frankenpandas output
+      description: Same shape summary for our output. Show the diff clearly.
+      render: text
+    validations:
+      required: true
+
+  - type: dropdown
+    id: level
+    attributes:
+      label: Expected parity level
+      description: |
+        MUST = pandas documents this behavior and users rely on it.
+        SHOULD = pandas does this by convention; divergence surprises users.
+        MAY = edge case with no clear contract; either behavior is defensible.
+      options:
+        - MUST — pandas docs the behavior
+        - SHOULD — convention / common usage
+        - MAY — edge case
+        - Unsure
+    validations:
+      required: true
+
+  - type: input
+    id: pandas_version
+    attributes:
+      label: pandas version used for comparison
+      placeholder: "pandas 2.2.3 (pinned in fp-conformance/oracle/requirements.txt)"
+    validations:
+      required: true
+
+  - type: checkboxes
+    id: precheck
+    attributes:
+      label: Pre-filing checks
+      options:
+        - label: I have checked `crates/fp-conformance/DISCREPANCIES.md` — this is NOT a documented intentional divergence.
+        - label: I have verified the pandas output on the pinned pandas version (not a pandas-between-versions difference).

@@ -0,0 +1,56 @@
+name: Feature Request
+description: Request a new pandas method or API surface
+title: "[FEATURE] "
+labels: ["enhancement", "triage"]
+body:
+  - type: markdown
+    attributes:
+      value: |
+        Thanks for the request. frankenpandas tracks pandas API parity, so feature requests almost always map to a specific pandas method. Please link the method docs.
+
+  - type: input
+    id: pandas_method
+    attributes:
+      label: pandas method name
+      placeholder: "pd.DataFrame.pivot_table / Series.str.extract / pd.merge_asof / ..."
+    validations:
+      required: true
+
+  - type: input
+    id: pandas_docs
+    attributes:
+      label: pandas docs URL
+      placeholder: "https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/reference/api/..."
+    validations:
+      required: true
+
+  - type: dropdown
+    id: crate
+    attributes:
+      label: Target crate (best guess)
+      options:
+        - fp-frame (DataFrame / Series core)
+        - fp-io (new IO format or option)
+        - fp-groupby
+        - fp-join
+        - fp-expr
+        - fp-index
+        - Other / unsure
+    validations:
+      required: true
+
+  - type: textarea
+    id: example
+    attributes:
+      label: Minimal motivating example
+      description: Show how you'd call the method. pandas-style snippet is fine; we'll translate the signature to Rust during design.
+      render: python
+    validations:
+      required: true
+
+  - type: textarea
+    id: signature_sketch
+    attributes:
+      label: Proposed Rust signature (optional)
+      description: If you have a preferred Rust API shape, share it. We'll use pandas semantics as the north star otherwise.
+      render: rust

@@ -0,0 +1,72 @@
+name: Performance Regression
+description: Report a significant slowdown or memory regression across versions
+title: "[PERF] "
+labels: ["perf", "regression", "triage"]
+body:
+  - type: markdown
+    attributes:
+      value: |
+        Perf regressions route through the br-ing6 perf baseline + xha7 differential-vs-pandas bench infrastructure. Include before/after numbers and ideally a bisect starting SHA so we can land a fix + regression gate.
+
+  - type: dropdown
+    id: operation
+    attributes:
+      label: Affected operation class
+      options:
+        - GroupBy (sum / mean / agg_named / agg_multi / apply)
+        - Rolling / Expanding / Ewm
+        - Merge / Join
+        - Arithmetic (series+series / df+df / align)
+        - IO (read_csv / read_parquet / read_excel / write_*)
+        - Indexing (loc / iloc / xs / set_index)
+        - Reshape (stack / unstack / pivot / pivot_table)
+        - Other
+    validations:
+      required: true
+
+  - type: textarea
+    id: before
+    attributes:
+      label: Before (baseline) timing
+      description: p50/p95/p99 from `perf_baselines.rs` run OR criterion bench OR a hand-timed measurement with the exact version / commit.
+      render: text
+    validations:
+      required: true
+
+  - type: textarea
+    id: after
+    attributes:
+      label: After (regression) timing
+      description: Same measurement on the slower commit. Same hardware / same input.
+      render: text
+    validations:
+      required: true
+
+  - type: input
+    id: bisect_good
+    attributes:
+      label: "Last known-good commit (if bisected)"
+      placeholder: "abc1234 / v0.1.0 / unknown"
+
+  - type: input
+    id: bisect_bad
+    attributes:
+      label: First known-bad commit (if bisected)
+      placeholder: "def5678 / v0.1.1 / unknown"
+
+  - type: textarea
+    id: reproducer
+    attributes:
+      label: Reproducer
+      description: Preferably a `#[bench]`-style closure or a `perf_baselines.rs`-compatible scenario.
+      render: rust
+    validations:
+      required: true
+
+  - type: textarea
+    id: hardware
+    attributes:
+      label: Hardware / environment
+      description: CPU model, cores, RAM, OS, Rust toolchain (cargo --version).
+    validations:
+      required: true
